LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. DEFENDANT DAVID ESCOBOSA'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS GRANTED. ON FEBRUARY 27, 2013, THE COURT ORDERED PLAINTIFF TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PURSUANT TO SAN FRANCISC LOCAL RULE 2.6B, AND WARNED THAT A FAILURE TO DO SO COULD RESULT IN THE MOTION BEING GRANTED. PLAINTIFF STILL HAS NOT COMPLIED WITH SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 2.6B BY PROVIDING A COURTESY COPY OF THE OPPOSITION. BASED ON THE PAPERS BEFORE THE COURT, THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff Anchor General Insurance Co.'S Motion To Compel Further Response To Form Interrogatories; And Request For Sanctions is Denied. Motion already has been continued once for moving party to provide courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers in accordance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. MINI MINUTES:

No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF ANCHOR GENERAL INSURANCE CO.'S Motion To Compel Further Response To Form Interrogatories; And Request For Sanctions is Continued to April 12, 2013 to give Plaintiff the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. No later than 4 p.m., April 4, 2013, Plaintiff must deliver courtesy copies of its moving and reply papers to Department 302 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date of April 12, 2013, or the motion is subject to being denied without further notice. The court has courtesy copies of the opposition. Pro Tem Judge: Ernst Halperin, Clerk: L. Fong, Not reported.

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Mar-15-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Hunter Sims III, Esq. of Stratman Patterson & Hunter, Tel. 510.457.3468, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT DAVID ESCOBOSA's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is Granted. On February 27, 2013, the Court ordered Plaintiff to provide courtesy copies of the opposition pursuant to San Francisc Local Rule 2.6B, and warned that a failure to do so could result in the motion being granted. Plaintiff still has not complied with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B by providing a courtesy copy of the opposition. Based on the papers before the Court, the Court grants the motion. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
